cards.title: Having Children Makes You Smarter
cards.body: Recent studies show mothers and fathers have healthier, younger-looking brains than non-parents. Years of mental and emotional challenges from parenting appear to boost cognitive health.

cards.title: I Wish My Son Wanted More Time With Me
cards.body: A parent seeks advice about their adult son's limited contact despite weekly touch-ins. An expert explores whether initial reluctance to parenthood might be creating unspoken pressure.

cards.title: Learning to Cope During Family Holidays
cards.body: A parent discovers they can manage real emergencies better than anticipated worries. Anxiety about worst-case scenarios faded when genuine crisis required action.

cards.title: Growing Children by Stepping Back
cards.body: Free play has quietly disappeared from modern childhood despite being crucial for healthy development. Psychology Today explores why unstructured time matters for kids.

cards.title: Your Anxious Child Isn't the Problem
cards.body: Reassuring anxious children about 'what if' worries actually trains them to ask more questions. Three evidence-based strategies can break this reinforcement cycle.

cards.title: When Your Child Is Spewing Sass
cards.body: Kids begin sassing as early as age 3, and big parental reactions often reinforce the behavior. A counterintuitive response proves most effective for correction.

cards.title: Boy, 3, Had Five Surgeries After Crocodile Attack
cards.body: A young child required multiple operations following a crocodile encounter. Parents face uncertainty about the toddler's future movement and sensation.

cards.title: Schools Starting Late After 1am England Kick-Off
cards.body: Some schools are opening late to reduce pressure on parents managing late-night sports events. The decision reflects schools balancing student schedules with family needs.

cards.title: Double Amputee Boy Inspires Everyone Around Him
cards.body: A young double amputee's courage and positivity has inspired his entire school community. The child demonstrates remarkable resilience in his recovery journey.

cards.title: Donor Siblings: Managing Concerns Thoughtfully
cards.body: Concerns about donor-conceived children having multiple biological siblings are common but manageable. Experts have developed strategies for families navigating this modern reality.
